original_image_tags: A news clipping featuring a sketch of a disc-shaped flying saucer. The object is dome-shaped with a wide, flat rim. It has three visible rounded rectangular "windows" or ports around the dome, and a small, lattice-like antenna or spire protruding from the top center. The saucer is depicted resting on or hovering very close to a flat surface, casting a shadow. The sketch is rendered in a sepia-toned, newsprint style.

elements.description: A newspaper clipping containing a hand-drawn sketch of a single domed flying saucer with triangular windows, small circular ports, a ringed base, and an antenna on top, hovering above a dark ground. The caption reads 'THIS IS IT A sketch of the flying saucer which Herbert Long, 29, a Kutztown, Pa., insurance salesman, contends he saw parked on a road 30 feet from his car. He said he was too frightened to approach it. He's shown (left, below) giving Leroy Gensler, artist, directions for the sketch. (AP Wirephotos)'.

judge.reason: Description A describes three rounded rectangular windows, while Description B describes triangular windows and small circular ports, contradicting the specific shape of the visual elements.
